question: Design a `Book` class with private members for title (string), author (string), and price (double). Include a parameterized constructor to initialize these members and a public method `displayBookInfo()` to print all details.

test_cases.input:
The Lord of the Rings
J.R.R. Tolkien
25.99
test_cases.expected:
Book Title: The Lord of the Rings
Book Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
Book Price: $25.99

Book Title: Dune
Book Author: Frank Herbert
Book Price: $15.75

Book Title: Pride and Prejudice
Book Author: Jane Austen
Book Price: $7.25

errors: The constructor parameters for title and author are passed by value (std::string), leading to unnecessary string copies and reduced efficiency.
The `displayBookInfo` method is not declared `const`, indicating that it might modify the object's state, which is not true and violates best practices for observer methods.
Constructor parameter names 't', 'a', 'p' are not descriptive and reduce readability.
